## Summary

Describe the change and the eMAS phase or component affected.

## Change type

- [ ] Governance, requirements or documentation
- [ ] PowerShell scripts or shared engine
- [ ] Mapping workbook or VBA
- [ ] Runtime JSON or schema
- [ ] Report template
- [ ] WPF interface
- [ ] Tests
- [ ] Build or release
- [ ] Supersession or archive update

## Authority and traceability

- Decision IDs:
- Decision-register Item IDs:
- Requirement IDs:
- Rule IDs:
- Test IDs:
- Configuration version:
- JSON schema version:
- Template version:
- Governing canonical documents:
- Change class:
- Required approver roles:

## Delivery state

- [ ] Decision approved
- [ ] Documentation synchronized
- [ ] Implementation complete
- [ ] Verification complete
- [ ] Release control complete
- [ ] Follow-up work is linked and explicitly pending

Do not mark a later state complete merely because the design decision is approved.

## Conflict check

- [ ] The canonical index and authority policy were reviewed.
- [ ] No lower-authority artifact overrides an approved governing source.
- [ ] No unresolved conflict affects regulatory interpretation, JSON compatibility, phase decisions, report meaning or evidence traceability.
- Conflict IDs, if any:
- Lower-authority artifacts requiring correction or supersession:

## Verification

- [ ] Change respects the authoring/runtime/execution source model.
- [ ] PowerShell does not read the mapping workbook or create, repair or reinterpret JSON.
- [ ] The same runtime JSON remains valid for all three phases.
- [ ] Source data remains read-only.
- [ ] Evaluation status remains separate from RAG.
- [ ] Findings remain separate from recommendations.
- [ ] Accepted exceptions do not erase observed findings.
- [ ] Controlled terminology is used.
- [ ] Relevant tests were added or updated.
- [ ] Documentation, canonical index and diagrams were updated where required.
- [ ] Superseded documents were registered where required.
- [ ] Binary workbook changes include reviewable VBA/source information where applicable.
- [ ] No customer data, credentials, production logs or project evidence are included.

## Approval evidence

Record required Product Owner, Regulatory SME, Migration SME, Technical Architect, PowerShell Lead, QA Lead, Corporate IT or Documentation Owner approval where applicable.

## Evidence

List commands executed, test results, generated sample evidence and known limitations.

## Follow-up work

List linked issues or pull requests for implementation, synchronization, testing, release or archive work that remains pending.
